design_philosophy.core_loop: Exploration, dialogue-driven quest resolution, and incremental memory recovery through interaction with NPCs and environmental storytelling across interconnected planes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: The mystery of The Nameless One's immortality curse and fragmented identity across multiple past lives versus the practical survival challenges in a hostile multiverse setting
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as an amnesiac immortal protagonist uncovering a personal epic spanning centuries, making moral choices that reshape faction relationships and personal alignment through dialogue and action
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radically prioritizes narrative and dialogue over combat; mortality is mechanically irrelevant (respawning is the expected mechanic); alignment system dynamically responds to player choices; many encounters explicitly designed to be resolved without violence; protagonist's unique amnesia framework inverts traditional RPG power fantasy into an introspective identity quest

difficulty.lives_system: Immortality mechanic: The Nameless One respawns when killed with no mechanical penalty beyond location change, inverting traditional permadeath tension
difficulty.checkpointing: Implicit checkpointing via respawn mechanic tied to immortality narrative
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through dialogue-based encounter resolution; many combat encounters can be avoided or resolved through dialogue and stealth; combat takes secondary role to player choice

progression.upgrade_system: Class change system (Fighter → Thief or Wizard mid-game); attribute allocation during creation; magical tattoo enhancement system for ability augmentation; spell and item acquisition through exploration and quest completion
progression.unlock_mechanism: Faction membership gates certain questlines and companion interactions; plane access unlocked through story progression; companion recruitment tied to narrative encounters and past-life connections
replayability_hooks: Seven potential companions with only five-slot party maximum creates different party configurations per playthrough
Alignment system (good/evil, law/chaos) branches NPC reactions and quest resolutions
Class flexibility allows Fighter → Thief or Wizard transformation affecting combat approach
Faction membership choices create distinct storyline branches

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through pre-rendered painted scenery depicting multiple planes (Sigil, Baator, Carceri)
NPC dialogue as primary narrative vehicle with frequent party member conversations
Journal system tracking numerous quests and subplots for player continuity
Planescape setting's faction system (law/chaos/good/evil) embedded in world's social structure
Companion backstories intertwined with The Nameless One's past lives, revealing history through relationships
Past-life memory fragments recovered through exploration and interaction
Portal-based plane-hopping structure reflecting multiverse cosmology

faction_indicators: Multiple joinable factions in Sigil, each controlling different city functions and worldviews
Alignment system (good/evil/law/chaos) tracks player ethical positioning and faction compatibility
NPC faction affiliation and reactions vary based on player alignment changes
danger_signals: Immortality curse itself represents metaphysical danger—resurrection without identity continuity
Multiple planes contain hostile creatures (devils, modrons) reflecting planar-specific threats
Lady of Pain governs Sigil with implied omnipotent oversight and severe punishments for rule-breaking
Past-life consequences manifest as NPCs with pre-existing grievances against previous incarnations
environmental_storytelling: Sigil's architectural design reflects faction control zones and planar connection points
The Mortuary (game opening location) establishes mortality mechanics visually
Portal descriptions and planar aesthetics communicate cosmological hierarchy
NPC clothing, dialogue patterns, and spatial positioning indicate faction membership
Decaying and pristine areas reflect plane-specific moral and physical properties
